procedure_name: High Risk Induction Chemotherapy for Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia
source_files: PMJAY_STG_Acute Myeloid Leukemia & Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: DM (Medical oncology) or equivalent (MD Medicine with experience/Fellowship in Hematology/Medical Oncology)
clinical_indications: All patients irrespective of age, co-morbidities, poor performance status
White blood cells >10 x 10^9/L (based on Sanz's Risk Stratification for High Risk)

clinical_thresholds.note: for high risk APML according to Sanz's Risk Stratification

preauth.label: FISH/RTPCR or cytogenetics confirmation of PML-RARA or other translocation involving RARA (within first 7 days for preauthorization)
Prior treatment with chemotherapy (if second time): regimen, dates, response (if applicable)

claim.label: Reports of tests supporting diagnosis (pathology, radiology, microbiology, hematology, biochemistry, etc.)
Discharge summary of day care department
Periodic CBC reports (once a week minimum during induction)
All documents as mentioned in preauthorization

ppd_preauth.q: FISH/CTG/PCR report for confirmation of APML attached?

cpd_claim.q: Same package re-claimed within 6 months for APML High Risk Induction?
Periodic CBC reports available for induction period?

common_queries: Confirmation of PML-RARA or other relevant translocation by FISH/RTPCR/cytogenetics missing within 7 days of pre-authorization.
Peripheral blood showing abnormal promyelocytes not documented.
Barcode of administered drugs or diagnosis supporting reports missing at claim stage.
Package re-claimed within 6 months without justification for APML High Risk Induction.
Patient's risk stratification (high risk) not clearly supported by WBC count.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Qualified Medical Oncologist/hematologist; Chemotherapy unit (Day care and Ward)
